items.title: Retrying a flaky model API call
items.scenario: An LLM provider occasionally returns transient 5xx errors. Wrapping the call site with @retry(times=3) re-issues the request a few times before giving up, so a single hiccup doesn't crash the request.
items.explanation: The decorator owns the loop and error handling, so every API helper gets the same retry policy without copy-pasting try/except blocks.

items.title: Caching identical embeddings
items.scenario: A pipeline embeds many documents, and the same boilerplate sentence appears thousands of times. @lru_cache stores the embedding for each unique string, so repeated inputs are returned instantly instead of re-calling the model.
items.explanation: This is safe only because embedding the same text is deterministic; the function is pure with respect to its input.

items.title: Registering tools for an agent
items.scenario: A framework discovers callable tools at startup. Authors mark a function with @tool, and the decorator appends it to a global registry the agent can later look up by name.
items.explanation: The decorator runs once at import time, turning a plain function definition into a side effect (registration) without the author writing any wiring code.
